title: DOM Manipulation Mastery
tagline: Create elements, animate, and build interactive components from scratch.

lessons.title: Create Elements Dynamically

accomplishments: Add, remove, and modify elements on the fly
Use textContent safely instead of innerHTML

do.title: Build a Dynamic Comment Section
steps: Copy the code — a complete comment system
Post comments that appear without refreshing
All built with createElement — no hardcoded HTML
do.result: You created HTML elements with JavaScript!

concepts.name: createElement()
concepts.definition: Creates a new HTML element in memory. Set properties, then appendChild to put it on the page.
concepts.analogy: Building a Lego piece in your hands before snapping it onto the board.

concepts.name: textContent vs innerHTML
concepts.definition: textContent sets plain text safely. innerHTML parses HTML — dangerous with user input (XSS). NEVER use innerHTML with user content.
concepts.analogy: textContent = writing on paper. innerHTML = giving someone a microphone — they could say anything.

concepts.name: insertBefore()
concepts.definition: Insert before a specific child. list.insertBefore(newItem, list.firstChild) adds to the beginning.
concepts.analogy: Cutting in line — you pick exactly where to insert.

concepts.name: Data Attributes
concepts.definition: Store custom data: element.dataset.id = '123'. HTML: data-id="123". Retrieve: element.dataset.id.
concepts.analogy: Name tags with extra info — elements carry their own metadata.

understand.keyTakeaway: createElement makes elements. appendChild adds them. textContent for safety. Data attributes for metadata.

build.hint: Create a reusable createTaskElement(text, id) function.

challenge.title: Build a Photo Gallery with Lightbox
challenge.mission: Dynamic thumbnails, click to open modal, prev/next navigation, keyboard support. All built with createElement.
requirements: Create thumbnails from array with createElement
challenge.starterCode:
const images = [{url: '...', title: '...'}];
// TODO: Build gallery and lightbox

bridge.preview: Next: querySelector, classList, DOM traversal, and event delegation.

lessons.devFession: I used innerHTML to render user comments. Someone typed <script>alert('hacked')</script> and it ran. textContent would have prevented it.

lessons.title: Manipulate the DOM Like a Pro

accomplishments: Build tabbed interfaces with event delegation

do.title: Build an Interactive Image Gallery
steps: Click thumbnails to change the main image
do.result: Gallery with querySelector, classList, event delegation, keyboard nav!
do.proofRequired: gallery with keyboard navigation and active states

concepts.name: querySelector
concepts.definition: Find elements with CSS selectors. querySelector('.btn.active') returns first match. querySelectorAll returns all.
concepts.analogy: CSS selectors in JavaScript — same syntax you already know.

concepts.name: classList
concepts.definition: .add(), .remove(), .toggle(), .contains(), .replace(). Modern way to manage CSS classes.
concepts.analogy: A class manager — add/remove labels without touching other classes.

concepts.name: DOM Traversal
concepts.definition: parentElement, children, firstElementChild, nextElementSibling, previousElementSibling, closest(selector).
concepts.analogy: Family tree navigation — go to parent, find siblings, visit children.

concepts.name: Event Delegation
concepts.definition: One listener on parent handles all children. parent.addEventListener('click', e => { if (e.target.matches('.item')) handle(e) }).
concepts.analogy: One receptionist for the whole building instead of a doorbell on every room.

understand.keyTakeaway: querySelector for finding. classList for classes. Traversal for navigation. Delegation for efficiency.

build.hint: Store panel ID in data-panel on each tab.

challenge.title: Build a Filterable Product Grid
challenge.mission: Products with category filters, sorting, search, event delegation.
requirements: 12+ products with image, name, price, category

bridge.preview: Next: animate elements with Intersection Observer and scroll triggers.

lessons.devFession: I added click listeners to 500 items individually. Event delegation: one listener on the parent. Performance went from laggy to instant.

lessons.title: Animate Elements with JavaScript

accomplishments: Build animated counters and progress bars

do.title: Build Scroll-Triggered Reveal Animations
steps: Watch elements fade in and slide up!
Uses Intersection Observer — modern and performant!
do.result: Scroll reveals, animated counters, and parallax — all performant!

concepts.name: Intersection Observer
concepts.definition: Detects when elements enter/leave viewport. Better than scroll events. threshold: 0.1 = trigger at 10% visible.
concepts.analogy: A security camera at a doorway — notices when someone enters, doesn't constantly check.

concepts.name: CSS Transitions + JS Classes
concepts.definition: CSS defines animation (.reveal { opacity: 0; transition: 0.8s }). JS adds the class (.reveal.active { opacity: 1 }). Best of both worlds.
concepts.analogy: CSS is the choreographer, JS is the stage manager who says 'go.'

concepts.name: requestAnimationFrame
concepts.definition: Syncs JS animations with browser repaints (60fps). Better than setInterval. Pauses when tab hidden.
concepts.analogy: Dancing to the beat — syncs your animation to the browser's rhythm.

concepts.name: Performance: transform + opacity
concepts.definition: Only animate these for 60fps — they're GPU-accelerated. Avoid width, height, top, left (trigger layout).
concepts.analogy: Moving a sticker on glass (transform) vs rebuilding the glass (layout).

understand.keyTakeaway: Intersection Observer for visibility. CSS for animations. requestAnimationFrame for smooth JS. Only animate transform and opacity.

build.goal: Build an Animated Landing Page with 5+ sections

codeComparison.new: observer.observe(element); // only fires on visibility change

build.hint: Set different transition-delay on cards for staggered effect.

challenge.title: Build an Interactive Portfolio
challenge.mission: Scroll progress bar, parallax hero, animated skill bars, project cards, smooth navigation.
challenge.bonusChallenge: Add dark/light mode with smooth transitions.

bridge.preview: Final lesson: build 5 essential UI components — modals, dropdowns, tabs, accordions, tooltips.

lessons.devFession: I used scroll listeners for 200 animations. 8fps. Intersection Observer: 60fps with less code.

lessons.title: Build Interactive Components

accomplishments: Build 5 essential UI components from scratch
Handle focus management and escape key

do.title: Build a Complete Component Library
steps: Try each: modal, dropdown, tabs, accordion, tooltip
do.result: 5 professional components with accessibility and keyboard support!

concepts.name: Modal Pattern
concepts.definition: Overlay that traps focus. Close on ESC and overlay click. Prevent background scroll. Focus first element on open.
concepts.analogy: A pop-up shop — demands attention, can't interact with store behind it.

concepts.name: Dropdown Pattern
concepts.definition: Menu below trigger. Closes on outside click (document listener checks closest('.dropdown')).
concepts.analogy: An umbrella — opens on click, closes when you tap anywhere else.

concepts.name: Tabs Pattern
concepts.definition: One panel at a time. Arrow keys navigate. ARIA: role=tablist, role=tab, aria-selected.
concepts.analogy: File folders — pull one out, others stay.

concepts.name: Focus Management
concepts.definition: Modal open → focus first element. Close → return to trigger. Tab trapping keeps focus inside.
concepts.analogy: A tour guide in a museum — directs you where to go and keeps you from wandering off.

concepts.name: ARIA Attributes
concepts.definition: Help screen readers: role (what it is), aria-label (name), aria-expanded (open/closed), aria-selected (active).
concepts.analogy: Labels for blind visitors describing what each element does.

understand.keyTakeaway: Components need focus management, keyboard support, and ARIA. Event delegation makes them scalable.

build.goal: Build an Image Carousel with controls, dots, auto-play, keyboard

codeComparison.new: openModal → trap focus → ESC close → overlay close → return focus

build.hint: Use transform: translateX() for smooth sliding.

challenge.mission: Dashboard combining all 5 component patterns plus sidebar nav and toast notifications.

lessons.devFession: I built a modal without ESC close, focus trapping, or background scroll lock. A screen reader user couldn't use my app. Accessibility isn't optional.
